name: Zerodha Session Analytics Engine
scenario: Zerodha's data team needs to analyze 1M daily trade events with rolling window metrics. Build a session analytics engine using sliding window algorithms.

core.description: maxVolumeWindow(int[] volumes, int k): max sum of k consecutive trade volumes. O(n) sliding window. Test: [3,1,2,5,8,2,1,4,3,6] k=3 → 15.
longestActiveSession(String events): longest substring of unique event types. O(n) variable window HashSet. Test: 'abcabcbb' → 3.
minWindowAllEvents(String s, String t): minimum window containing all event types in t. O(n). Test: 'ADOBECODEBANC','ABC' → 'BANC'.
findPatternOccurrences(String s, String p): all start indices where p's anagram occurs. O(n). Test: 'cbaebabacd','abc' → [0,6].
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): maxVolume=15, session=3, minWindow='BANC', anagrams=[0,6]. All pass.
README: explains why variable sliding window is O(n) despite nested while loop (amortized lo movements).

bonus.description: minSubarrayLen(target, nums): min length subarray sum >= target. Test: [2,3,1,2,4,3] target=7 → 2.
atMostKDistinct(String s, int k): longest substring with at most k distinct chars. Test: 'eceba' k=2 → 3.
characterReplacement(String s, int k): longest repeating char with k replacements. Test: 'AABABBA' k=1 → 4.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Fixed window: build first k elements, then slide by adding nums[i] - nums[i-k] each step. Variable window: expand right always (for hi), shrink left (lo++) when window is INVALID. lo is monotonically non-decreasing — total lo movements ≤ n. Frequency array vs HashMap: for lowercase/uppercase alpha, use int[26] or int[128] (O(1) comparison). For arbitrary values use HashMap. minWindow contains all of t: use 'required' counter — decrements when a needed char is satisfied (not just present). Anagram check: compare two int[26] arrays — O(26) = O(1).
